source_jobs.title: Field Partnerships Sales Lead - Robotics & AI Data Collection
source_jobs.description:
About the Role 
Appen is looking for motivated, people-oriented Field Operations Specialists to support a time-sensitive AI data collection program. This data collection is geared towards capturing real-world physical tasks that can be used in robotics training. You will operate as an on-the-ground representative for Appen, signing up commercial businesses to paid AI data programs, and guiding them through onboarding and setup. This is a hands-on, field-based role that combines sales skills, relationship management, training, and reporting. 
What You’ll Do 
Business Outreach & Onboarding 
Canvas assigned commercial areas, visiting businesses across hospitality, retail, manufacturing, cleaning, construction, and other workplace settings.

source_jobs.title: Office & Administration Coordinator -1 Year Fixed Term Contract
source_jobs.description:
Role Purpose
This role keeps our secure facility running smoothly, safely, and compliantly. The purpose of this role is to own day-to-day office operations, health and safety compliance, and client order administration in a structured, low-distraction environment. You will work independently, using your own initiative, with minimal oversight.
Your Impact
Maintain a safe, compliant, and well-organised workplace through active management of H&S obligations, risk assessments, and fire safety procedures.
Coordinate office supplies, maintenance, and contractor relationships to keep operations running without interruption.
Process client orders accurately and on time, including audio quality checks and secure return of completed work.
Identify issues early and resolve them proactively, escalating only where necessary.
Uphold strict confidentiality and data protection standards across all activities.
What You Bring
Minimum 2 years in office operations, facilities coordination, or a similar operational support role.
Solid working knowledge of health and safety requirements, including risk assessments, fire safety, and compliance schedules.
Confident IT skills and a track record of picking up new systems and processes quickly.
High attention to detail, particularly in data entry, compliance records, and sensitive information handling.
Strong communication skills for dealing with colleagues, suppliers, and contractors professionally.
A resourceful, problem-solving mindset with the ability to work independently and get things done.
Security clearance eligibility (DBS and SC), funded by the company.

source_jobs.title: Project Specialist
source_jobs.description:
Role Purpose
Appen's GenAI Project Delivery team sits at the intersection of AI development and real-world data quality, executing the annotation, evaluation, and data collection work that shapes how frontier models perform. The purpose of this role is to drive end-to-end delivery excellence across GenAI projects by ensuring task configuration, contributor performance, and data quality outcomes stay tightly aligned to client expectations. This is a hands-on, cross-functional role with influence over delivery standards, client outcomes, and the continuous improvement of how Appen executes at scale.

source_jobs.title: Workforce Manager- On-Site (Night Shift)
source_jobs.description:
About the Role
 
As Workforce Manager for Project Nectar, you will be the data-driven force behind how our night shift runs at our Cavite facility. Working alongside our Supervisors and Annotators, you will use real-time performance data and forecasted demand to guide staffing decisions during peak hours, protect service levels, and keep the team positioned for success.
 
This is not a traditional people-management role. Our Supervisors already handle day-to-day floor support, so instead of managing individuals directly, you will provide the analytical insight and direction that keeps a 200+ person team performing at its best, and you will be a key point of contact for workforce performance with both internal leadership and the client.
 
What You'll Do
 
—Translate demand forecasts into staffing plans, shift rosters, and schedules for a 200+ person team.
—Monitor real-time queues and service levels, making live decisions on breaks, reallocation, and skill-based routing to protect SLAs.
—Guide and direct Team Leaders and Supervisors on forecasting and schedules.
—Track and report on core workforce KPIs, including Service Level, Occupancy, Utilization, Shrinkage, Adherence, AHT, Attrition, and Absenteeism.
—Build dashboards and reports that give leadership clear visibility into shift and project performance.
—Identify the trends and root causes behind KPI shifts, and drive corrective action plans.
—Act as the primary point of contact for workforce matters on your shift, liaising with client stakeholders and internal teams such as HR, IT, and Facilities.
 
What You'll Bring
 
—2 to 4+ years of experience in Workforce Management, Operations, or Shift Management within a BPO/contact center or large-scale operations environment.
—Proven experience managing forecasting, scheduling, and real-time management for a workforce of 150+ employees, including peak-hour surges.
—Strong Excel and data analysis skills; experience with BI tools such as Power BI or Tableau is a plus.
—Experience influencing or guiding Team Leaders and Supervisors without needing formal management authority.
—Willingness to work on-site in Cavite on a night shift, with flexibility for rotating hours as demand shifts.
 
Nice to Have
 
—Experience on outsourced or managed services projects.
—Experience in a start-up or ramp-up project environment, including new project mobilisation.
—Experience working directly with international or offshore clients.

notes: Appen's job postings suggest a focus on operations and project management, with no explicit tech roles.
